text.title: Heuristic Evaluation Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding Heuristic Evaluation. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: Three to five qualified evaluators work independently before consolidating; fewer misses issues, more produces diminishing returns.

rationale.source: Nielsen — How to Conduct a Heuristic Evaluation

rule.rationale: Below three the coverage is poor; above five the marginal cost outweighs new findings.

rule.statement: Severity is rated on a 1-to-4 scale tied to task-completion impact, not aesthetic preference.
rule.rationale: Without a rubric, severity collapses to opinion.

rule.statement: Every finding cites which of the ten Nielsen heuristics it violates.
rule.rationale: Heuristic citations make findings auditable and trainable.

rule.statement: Evaluators work independently first, then merge findings; merging before independent passes biases toward the loudest evaluator.
Findings include a recommended fix and reference to the design-system component to update.
rule.rationale: Findings without fixes rarely get implemented.

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-heuristic-evaluation.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
